root_text.an5.72:1.1: “Pañcime, bhikkhave, dhammā bhāvitā bahulīkatā cetovimuttiphalā ca honti cetovimuttiphalānisaṁsā ca, paññāvimuttiphalā ca honti paññāvimuttiphalānisaṁsā ca.
root_text.an5.72:1.3: Aniccasaññā, anicce dukkhasaññā, dukkhe anattasaññā, pahānasaññā, virāgasaññā—
root_text.an5.72:1.4: ime kho, bhikkhave, pañca dhammā bhāvitā bahulīkatā cetovimuttiphalā ca honti cetovimuttiphalānisaṁsā ca, paññāvimuttiphalā ca honti paññāvimuttiphalānisaṁsā ca.
root_text.an5.72:1.5: Yato kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu cetovimutto ca hoti paññāvimutto ca—
root_text.an5.72:1.6: ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, ‘bhikkhu ukkhittapaligho itipi, saṅkiṇṇaparikho itipi, abbūḷhesiko itipi, niraggaḷo itipi, ariyo pannaddhajo pannabhāro visaṁyutto itipi’”.
root_text.an5.72:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno avijjā pahīnā hoti ucchinnamūlā tālāvatthukatā anabhāvaṅkatā āyatiṁ anuppādadhammā.
root_text.an5.72:2.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ukkhittapaligho hoti.
root_text.an5.72:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno ponobhaviko jātisaṁsāro pahīno hoti ucchinnamūlo tālāvatthukato anabhāvaṅkato āyatiṁ anuppādadhammo.
root_text.an5.72:3.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu saṅkiṇṇaparikho hoti.
root_text.an5.72:4.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno taṇhā pahīnā hoti ucchinnamūlā tālāvatthukatā anabhāvaṅkatā āyatiṁ anuppādadhammā.
root_text.an5.72:4.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu abbūḷhesiko hoti.
root_text.an5.72:5.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno pañcorambhāgiyāni saṁyojanāni pahīnāni honti ucchinnamūlāni tālāvatthukatāni anabhāvaṅkatāni āyatiṁ anuppādadhammāni.
root_text.an5.72:5.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu niraggaḷo hoti.
root_text.an5.72:6.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ariyo pannaddhajo pannabhāro visaṁyutto hoti?
root_text.an5.72:6.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno asmimāno pahīno hoti ucchinnamūlo tālāvatthukato anabhāvaṅkato āyatiṁ anuppādadhammo.
root_text.an5.72:6.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ariyo pannaddhajo pannabhāro visaṁyutto hotī”ti.

translation_text.an5.72:0.3: Freedom of Heart is the Fruit (2nd)
translation_text.an5.72:1.1: “Mendicants, these five things, when developed and cultivated, have freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom as their fruit and benefit.
translation_text.an5.72:1.3: The perception of impermanence, the perception of suffering in impermanence, the perception of not-self in suffering, the perception of giving up, and the perception of fading away.
translation_text.an5.72:1.4: These five things, when developed and cultivated, have freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom as their fruit and benefit.
translation_text.an5.72:1.5: When a mendicant has freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom,
translation_text.an5.72:1.6: such a mendicant is one who is called ‘one who has lifted the cross-bar’, ‘one who has filled in the moat’, ‘one who has pulled up the pillar’, ‘one who is unimpeded’, and also ‘a noble one with banner lowered and burden dropped, detached’. …”
